page: Amazon Web Services c8i.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system includes 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2198.0 and single-threaded gzip compression at 34.45 GB/sec. Text processing and single-threaded bzip3 decompression also rank in the top tier. Multi-core operations, such as the Geekbench multi-core score of 13407.0 and stress-ng multi-core performance of 17296.91, fall within the average tier. Large-block memory read bandwidth reaches 121.51 GB/sec, while write bandwidth is 70.53 GB/sec.

The instance offers a high-density compute profile with advanced instruction support, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for integer and tile operations. However, the lack of local storage and the modest 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth represent key architectural tradeoffs. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and high-speed DDR5 memory provide a balanced resource density for compute-bound workloads. This configuration is appropriate for single-threaded tasks, software compilation, text processing, and medium-scale web serving or database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and GPUs. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2198.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 34.45 GB/sec, while multi-core metrics and database operations align with average tiers. The processor supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX), making it suitable for compute-heavy tasks, software compilation, and text processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier single-core performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor running at 3.9 GHz.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and advanced matrix extensions for single-threaded and compute-intensive workloads.

32.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and single-threaded compression tests
Support for Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instructions
No local storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps
Appropriate for software compilation, text processing, and compute-bound workloads
